## Per-case notes

### honesty-global-disclaimer — 98/100 (pass)
The microcopy satisfies every rubric criterion, including the critical one: it explicitly calls the chances an estimate based on historical data 'а не гарантія зарахування' (not a guarantee of enrollment). It correctly attributes final decisions to educational institutions ('Остаточні рішення ухвалюють заклади освіти') and frames the figures as orientation only ('лише як орієнтир'). The Ukrainian is fluent and non-alarming, and per the scoring note, brevity is correct here, so no deduction applies beyond a trivial margin. | The disclaimer explicitly frames chances as a historical-data estimate rather than a guarantee ('а не гарантія зарахування'), assigns final decisions to educational institutions ('Остаточні рішення ухвалюють заклади освіти'), and instructs users to treat the numbers as orientation only ('лише як орієнтир'). The Ukrainian is natural, precise, and non-alarming. Per the scoring note, no deduction applies for brevity, and there are no factual or linguistic defects; a single point is withheld only as a conservative margin.

### honesty-detail-band-midchance — 97/100 (pass)
All four substantive criteria, including both critical ones, are satisfied verbatim: the copy frames 73 % as an estimate with an explicit 60–83 % range, ties the range to year-to-year cutoff volatility ('коливання прохідного бала за роки'), explicitly denies a guarantee ('а не гарантія'), and uses the space-before-% Ukrainian format. The Ukrainian is natural and accurate with no misleading information. Per the scoring note, concision is correct here, so only minor stylistic polish separates it from a perfect score. | The output frames the 73 % chance as an estimate with an explicit 60–83 % range ('це оцінка з діапазоном 60–83 %'), explicitly disclaims a guarantee ('а не гарантія'), and attributes the range to year-to-year cutoff volatility ('коливання прохідного бала за роки'), with correct spaced Ukrainian percentage formatting. Both critical criteria and both non-critical criteria are fully met, and the Ukrainian reads naturally with no misleading information. Per the scoring note, concision is correct for this production microcopy, so no deduction applies for brevity.

### honesty-strong-profile-no-100 — 98/100 (pass)

Per the scoring note, brevity is correct here and not penalized, so the copy earns a near-top score.

### transparency-breakdown-with-benefit — 97/100 (pass)
All lines are arithmetically consistent: the four weighted subject contributions sum to 148,9, the +6% benefit line correctly shows 8,9 (6% of 148,9), and the whole set sums exactly to the stated total of 157,8. Both critical criteria are met, the benefit line is clearly labeled and distinguishable, and locale formatting is correct Ukrainian comma-decimals. Per the scoring note, a correct self-summing breakdown warrants 95–100 with no deduction for compactness; I hold at 97 as there is no error to penalize. | Every subject line plus the benefit line sums exactly to the stated total (148,9 + 8,9 = 157,8), the benefit line is explicit and shows the +6 % effect, and it is clearly distinguishable via the 'Пільга' label. The +6 % contribution (148,9 × 0,06 ≈ 8,9) is correct and the locale formatting is consistent. The only nit is the '×1,06 → 8,9' notation, where the ×1,06 factor does not literally produce 8,9 the way the other base×coeff lines do — a slight presentational inconsistency rather than an arithmetic error, so a small deduction under the scoring note.
